paragraphs.body: Definition: Having a uniform surface that isn’t rough. It originates from a Middle English word form from old English smoõ meaning free or roughness, polished, or soft. This word can also describe something that happens without resistance or difficulty. A saying such as “that was smooth” or “smooth sailing”. This version of the word originates from the Proto-German word “smothaz”. Overtime, this saying came to include things such as actions and not just texture.

annotations.comment: I'm interested in this etymological exploration! I wonder, though, if it's the best way to begin your essay? Let's see :)
annotations.context_left: ord “smothaz”. Overtime, this saying came to include things such as actions and not just texture.

paragraphs.body: The word smooth has evolved in drastic ways over centuries. Initially, smooth was used to describe the physical texture of objects and surfaces. In later dates though, it began to take on a metaphorical implication In certain contexts. The social implications of “smooth” are also used to describe human interactions. The action of “smoothness” or “being smooth” was influenced by types of art like literature, painting, etc. As these types of things could be refined pieces of worth, smooth became a compliment for them. This is also why today you hear the terms like “smooth jazz”.
The word has many positive connotations like just explained. Smooth can mean to be effortless, calm, sophisticated, polished, refined, or pleasing.

I try to embody every single one of those words. I like to be “smooth”. It is funny enough though, still when I think of smooth, I think of objects that have an effortless touch. One thing that comes to mind is the cap of the Louis Vuitton Symphony bottle is big in comparison to most other fragrance bottle caps. It is a sculptural marvel made of some shiny silver material. It is as smooth as a river stone yet is weighted down with intention. It gleams and glistens under light, reflecting silvers like a reflection in a mirror. The surface is as sleek as ice, with unpredictable curves and edges that seamlessly flow into each other like a river with a laminar flow. The cap has a surprising weight to it when it is in your hand. It has the same weight as one of those small water bottles. Running your fingertip across its surface, there is no resistance, only an uninterrupted glide until your finger comes off of the cap. The cap finds its way onto the bottle seamlessly, as you guide the gap closer, the weight lets you drop the cap onto the bottle as it magnetically snaps together and is now connected to its home base. On impact, you feel the vibration of the heavy cap hitting the solid bottle and the magnetic seal closing, and every time, there is a dense click sound, letting you know that the objects aren’t hollow. When lifted, there is a moment of tension as you are breaking that magnetic seal, like opening a fridge door. The cap possesses a smell of the fragrance it is connected to, simple yet elegant, it smells like a beautiful blend of juicy grapefruit, fresh bergamot, and Inviting ginger. The cap looks as if you were to take molten silver from a bucket and start pouring it out from above, and it hardened in an instant, building on top of each other quickly.

annotations.text: It is a sculptural marvel made of some shiny silver material. It is as smooth as a river stone yet is weighted down with intention.
annotations.comment: Fascinating description.  I wonder what the "intention" is?
Why? What do you mean? Let's explore this :)

annotations.text: it smells like a beautiful blend of juicy grapefruit, fresh bergamot, and Inviting ginger.

paragraphs.body: Upon actually getting to grasp this bottle, the cap magnetically was snapped into place. The resistance was noticeable. Elegant and classy. It was like a door sealing shut a secret that hadn’t been revealed yet. I picked up the bottle, with the weight being unexpected and cool to the touch. I saw my fingerprint embossing itself into the bottle as I repositioned it in my hand. This was my first time being able to get my hands on this fragrance, and besides the actual lavender juice inside that I was excited to smell, the cap of the bottle was fascinating. The room was fancy, but this bottle was fancier. It is like the room was positioned for his bottle to be main stage gleaming in all of the light and glory. It was so unfamiliar yet inviting. It glistened and shined with a design I can’t describe as any way other than silver being poured out and hardened instantly as it landed. Abstract yet sleek. I broke the magnetic seal of the bottle and was hit with the most pleasant aroma that can only be described as a beautifully vibrant and juicy blend of grapefruit, bergamot, and ginger. It’s almost like I could see its lavender silage roaming in the air. When I was done, I placed the cap back on, now in a different position than it once was, and let the weight of the cap re-align itself with the bottle from the magnetic seal it has. The weight of the cap and the bottle now led my hand to land it back on its stand for others to admire.

annotations.text: Upon actually getting to grasp this bottle, the cap magnetically was snapped into place. The resistance was noticeable.
annotations.comment: Why is this essay in the passive construction? Who is snapping the bottle into place?
I wonder if, after we figure out what "smooth" means in terms of your essay's central focusing idea, if there might be a narrative paragraph to write about how and why being "smooth" matters to you?
annotations.context_left: weight of the cap and the bottle now led my hand to land it back on its stand for others to admire.

paragraphs.body: The fragrance is called Symphony. I find that interesting because a symphony is not just a song or a melody. It is not a single piece of music you listen to or can hum by yourself. It is not just a noise, even though it is quite vibrant and loud. It also isn’t in the background. I’ve never heard somebody say they heard a symphony as background noise. A symphony doesn’t even have to be music. It can be referenced as a bunch of things working in harmony to make something full or complete. Something like this doesn’t happen out of the blue. It’s constructed and put together over time. A symphony takes time to build and time to completely understand. A symphony is many things coming together in unison to become one. You put together an orchestra with all the different instruments playing together in perfect harmony and now you have a symphony. In other instances though, it can be events or actions being put together like a puzzle that have nothing to do with music, but when put together, they are vibrant and loud, yet elegant, like a symphony is. In my writing, the symphony I have made up is one of events and objects that all intertwine to make up a whole that works fluently together. This whole is that every detail of what I’ve written about defines a piece of me and who I am as a person. I want to be smooth, good at everything I do, and be the elegance in the room that people aspire to be.

annotations.text: I’ve never heard somebody say they heard a symphony as background noise.
annotations.comment: Interesting. What's the potential metaphor here? Speaking of metaphors, what _is_ the metaphor of the bottle?

paragraphs.body: What I find most fascinating about “smooth” is that other things that you think are also smooth even in a different way actually define the word in all of the other definitions it has. The cap is smooth as in lack of texture but it is also smooth as it takes the center of attention in a room looking elegant while doing so. It has a status and a power to it. All of the positive connotations of smooth is what it has. Everybody wants what this cap has. I said in the beginning of this writing that I wanted to me all the positive connotations of smooth, but so is this little bottle cap. This single object embodies everything I want to be. It has taken all the attributes I want to be and has smoothly combined them all into one inanimate object, the Louis Vuitton Symphony cap. Work Cited:
